mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased earnings expectations and consumption-driven GDP growth over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 137410 thousand suggests ongoing employment growth trajectory
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with labor market tightening
failure_condition: Payroll growth acceleration does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances where PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds 0.15%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.26% indicates restrictive policy stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation control priority
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns is positive (r > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through a demand-destruction channel.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change < -1.0% AND HOUST month-over-month change < -2.0% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 370495 million and HOUST at 1408 thousand show moderate demand levels
failure_condition: Negative 60-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST contract simultaneously, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 0.3% month-over-month signals expanding productive capacity and manufacturing strength, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over the next 5 trading days through a supply-side efficiency channel.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 111.9282 reflects post-crisis industrial recovery
failure_condition: Positive 5-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) signals the Fed's commitment to monetary tightening, which increases real discount rates and produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 203.153 and FEDFUNDS at 5.26% indicate inflation-fighting monetary stance
regime_hint: inflation-fighting tightening cycle with growth headwinds
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.3% and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.8% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses equity valuations through lower earnings expectations and consumption growth over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.5% remains below trigger threshold but shows vulnerability to shocks
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 60-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 4.8%, or correlation between UNRATE level and 60-day forward returns is positive (r > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) growth above 1.5% month-over-month signals strong residential investment and forward-looking demand expectations, which supports construction employment and durable goods consumption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1408 thousand reflects post-crisis housing recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-recession demand recovery with housing market strength
failure_condition: Positive 20-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 54% of instances when HOUST month-over-month growth exceeds 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at rates below 2.0% annualized signals economic stagnation and reduced corporate earnings growth, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations compress and equity risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11506.5 billion chained 2017 dollars reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Negative 60-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances when GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
